title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective pattern selection and coupling decision record; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in pattern selection and coupling decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
Order the Broker Fit Boundary RabbitMQ and TypeScript messaging evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Broker Fit Boundary assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, keep Broker Fit Boundary connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A debug messaging team handles case 3474 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Broker Fit Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Broker Fit Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug messaging team handles case 7426 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieving Broker Fit Boundary through the concrete pattern selection and coupling decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, keep Broker Fit Boundary connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Broker Fit Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug messaging team handles case 7426 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: RabbitMQ queues are a durable event log and should retain all history indefinitely.
